# uptimepage_status_page (Resource)

A public status page: its own subdomain slug, branding, and a curated set of monitors (added with uptimepage_status_page_component).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "uptimepage_status_page" "public" {
  slug    = "acme"
  name    = "Acme Status"
  enabled = true

  display_name    = "Acme Status"
  about           = "Live status of Acme's public services."
  brand_color     = "#0a7cff"
  style           = "default"
  show_powered_by = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Operator-facing page name.
- `slug` (String) Globally-unique slug that routes the public page's subdomain. Lowercased by the API.

### Optional

- `about` (String) Public 'about' blurb shown on the page.
- `brand_color` (String) Accent color as a 6-digit hex like `#3b82f6`. Defaults to the configured brand color when unset.
- `display_name` (String) Public header name. Falls back to the org name when unset.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the page is published (publicly reachable).
- `show_powered_by` (Boolean) Pin the 'powered by' footer on or off. Omit to inherit the deployment default.
- `style` (String) Visual theme.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Status page id (UUID).
- `logo_url` (String) Versioned public logo URL, or null when no logo. Upload via the UI.
- `status_url` (String) Live public URL of the page, or null when no public surface is mounted.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Status pages are imported by their id (UUID).
terraform import uptimepage_status_page.public 0192f3a4-5b6c-7d8e-9f01-23456789abcd
```
